note: Wind-down amendment to an existing JV — terminates Veoneer parties' involvement in two JVs (VNBJ, VNBZ), preserves Autoliv AB's role in ANRA. This is a JV-related document but not a fresh JV.

items.risk: No general release of pre-closing claims — Veoneer parties cease to be parties but liability for pre-closing breach SURVIVES
Conditional effectiveness on TWO closings (VNBJ + VNBZ) — what if only one closes? Document is ambiguous
ANRA wind-down obligations continue indefinitely for Nissin + Autoliv AB
D&O indemnification carve-out continues but only for pre-closing liabilities
No governing law in the Amendment itself — inherits from underlying JV which is not attached
Tail liability for representations + warranties + covenants — pre-closing only, narrow
No specified mechanism for resolving disputes about whether a breach was pre- or post-closing

qualitative_tone: should flag survival of pre-closing liability as the single biggest risk. Synthesis should be SHORT because the doc is narrow — overproducing concerns on a wind-down is a sign of generic blather. A junior would flag every word; a senior would flag 2-3 things.
